pattern: I am / You are / He is
example: She is a university student.
We study English every evening.
He works in a small office.
Does she live near here?
There are two cafes near my house.
I can finish this task tonight.
They are preparing for the exam.

pattern: some / any with countable and uncountable nouns
example: Do you have any free time this afternoon?
I watched a documentary last night.
She went to Da Nang last weekend.
I am going to apply for that course.
I think it will rain this evening.
This route is faster than the old one.
I have completed three practice tests.
You should review your notes every day.

pattern: First Conditional: If + present, will + V
example: If I finish early, I will join the meeting.
I have visited Hue, but I went there years ago.
She has worked here for five years.

pattern: Second Conditional: If + past, would + V
example: If I had more time, I would learn Spanish.
The report was submitted on Monday.
The book that I bought is very useful.
He said that he needed more details.

pattern: Modals of deduction: must / might / can't
example: She must be busy because she did not reply.
Although it was late, we still finished the task.
